prompt: I am just getting started. Why is single-pixel imaging interesting, and what kinds of applications does this review emphasize?
assistant_content: Single-pixel imaging is interesting because it offers a cost-effective alternative to conventional camera sensors across a broad wavelength range. The review highlights applications in microscopy, remote sensing, quantum state tomography, and other multidimensional sensing settings where sparsity makes measurement more efficient.

prompt: I am new to this paper. What are RVT and APR doing here, in simple terms?
assistant_content: From the retrieved method evidence, in simple terms: RVT converts each pinhole image into a radial-symmetry map so registration is more robust to interferometric phase, and APR uses phase-correlation registration to estimate shift vectors before applying those shifts back to the original iISM data for summation.

segments.primary_heading_path: Interferometric Image Scanning Microscopy for label-free imaging at 120 nm lateral resolution inside live cells / Data analysis / Contrast
segments.anchor_text: From the retrieved method evidence, in simple terms:

segments.text: RVT converts each pinhole image into a radial-symmetry map so registration is more robust to interferometric phase.
segments.raw_markdown: RVT converts each pinhole image into a radial-symmetry map so registration is more robust to interferometric phase.
segments.primary_heading_path: Interferometric Image Scanning Microscopy for label-free imaging at 120 nm lateral resolution inside live cells / Results / iISM with adaptive pixel-reassignment (APR) / Figure 2
segments.anchor_text: Specifically, we use the radial variance transform (RVT), which converts an interferogram into an intensity-only map that reflects local symmetry.

segments.text: APR uses phase-correlation registration to estimate shift vectors before applying those shifts back to the original iISM data for summation.
segments.raw_markdown: APR uses phase-correlation registration to estimate shift vectors before applying those shifts back to the original iISM data for summation.
segments.primary_heading_path: Interferometric Image Scanning Microscopy for label-free imaging at 120 nm lateral resolution inside live cells / Data analysis / Adaptive pixel-reassignment (APR)
segments.anchor_text: APR was performed using image registration based on phase correlation of the off-axis raw images and the resulting shifts were applied back to the original iISM pinhole stack.

hits.text: RVT converts an interferogram into an intensity-only map, and APR uses phase-correlation registration to estimate shifts before summation.

prompt: From the Discussion section only, what future directions or extensions do the authors suggest for iISM?
assistant_content: The discussion suggests extending iISM with SPAD-array detection and parallelized detection schemes so imaging can run at higher temporal resolution and reach dynamic processes on the millisecond scale.

prompt: From the Discussion section only, what limitation do the authors note about calibrating different SPAD arrays, and what follow-up do they suggest?
assistant_content: The discussion says the calibrated noise parameters can vary across different SPAD arrays, so automatic calibration remains future work. It also suggests transfer learning as a way to adapt the approach to other single-photon hardware and reduce repeated data collection and parameter calibration.
